annotation.text:
Waiting
Waiting
Because you look for me… (unintelligible)

“Que yo me voy para bomba” Erimar Benítez Burgos
Chorus

Ay, I’m heading to bomba
And I’m going to enjoy myself
Ay, and it delights me to sing my rhythm
For my drum… (unintelligible)
(unintelligible)
Because I want to begin
And I tell myself the six in a row everywhere
Ay, I’m heading to bomba
And bomba, my bomba
Ay, ay, ay
Ay, ay, ay

tags.tag: Que yo me voy pa la bomba

children.text: "Oi, how my bomba sounds, it is my voice" Erimar Benítez Burgos

annotation.text:
Dance my bomba
Dance this rhythm
Keep going
Dance
Keep going
Dance my bomba
Dance (unintelligible)
Dance (unintelligible)
Sing
It’s from Loíza
Listen to how my barrels (drums) sound

Listen, to how the Caribbean sounds!
When my barrel sounds everything can… (unintelligible)

How the barrels sound, everything can… (unintelligible)
If you breathe the bomba, then you can… (respond)

"Una bomba" Cindy Enid Cruzando Dominguez
Chorus

A bomba for what is coming
A bomba for what is here
A bomba for those who came before
In my mind it will live on!
A bomba for those who came before
In my heart it remains.

Barrel drummer, you are playing
The rhythm… (unintelligible)… breathing
This is my song

Long live the Candelaria of the (ladrona?) of Mayagüez
Long live the Candelaria, for the candle I will sing!

“Que yo me voy pa’ monte” Lleana Vázquez Castro
Chorus

I’m going to the mountains
I’m going to the sea
I’m going to the river
I’m going to heal myself

tags.tag: Que yo me voy pa' la monte

annotation.text: "Hay que linda la semilla"  Karyn M. Tarrats Morales
How beautiful the seed is that (unintelligible) that was planted

“Congo está llamando” Ángel D. Guilbe
Verses

It is in me
In my chest
Calling me
It is inside
Congo calls
And I answer it
With this song
That is inside
Congo calls

Congo is calling
Congo is calling
Congo asks for bomba and I am already responding with rhythm

“Baila mi tambor” Nelie Lebrón Robles
Chorus

“Donde pica la ola” Yasmin M. Vicente Gómez
Chorus

From every curved breath  
From whatever story of the friend  
These forbidden verses  
It bites me… (line incomplete)  
From every field of sargassum  
In paradise  
Here is my sail in its overflow  
I bite at its lap  
It is happy in the sand  
There was more blood in your sorrow  
This is how my burning stings  
Your skin is always brown  
It’s that if you hear its song  
It is smoke from its cloak  
It’s that if your crying comes close  
With a dream… (unintelligible) … broken  
It is happy in the sand  
In the blood of your veins  
It’s only how my burning stings  
Your skin is always brown  
It’s that my water enters  
Hope grows  
The ceremony in your mind  
From which the skin hardens  
Innocence will defend itself  
Patience will defend itself  
There is a mystery in its absence  
Consciousness can be seen  
… it grows  
Its (rona?) the earth that understands  
It is what ignites in my soul  
Life growing
I will wake up one day, wake up one day, wake up one day in a land of ours!

I returned to my homeland
I returned to being myself
I returned to being a cimarrón
I returned to singing
I returned to dancing
To flirting
I returned to healing
Oh yes
I returned, returned
Oh, how I returned
And that I returned
To be very happy
I returned
I returned
I returned
I returned
Yes, I returned
I returned to being happy
I returned to the river
I returned to the sea
I returned to adore
I returned to heal
I returned to return
Yes, I returned
To do what I gave
I returned for me
I returned for you
Yes, I returned
So I returned
I returned to heal
I returned to play
I returned to dance
I returned to things, to place
I returned to return
To be myself, I gave you
I returned, returned
Yes, I returned
To be what I gave
To flirt again
I returned to sing
To things and more
I returned, returned, returned
Yes, I returned
To be happy
Yes, I returned
To sing
To flirt
To dance
I returned, returned
To be happy
“Yo soy lo que le rompe la tierra” Ilia Correa Sepúlveda

I am the one who breaks the land
From the Caribbean, from the soul
I am of the land

I am Caribbean
I sing bomba
I am from the Ayalas of Loíza
I am from the Cepedas
From my homeland, Borinquen

tags.tag: Yo soy lo que le rompe la tierra
